title: Cough and Cold Relief with 9 Herbal Tinctures

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for cough because it helps reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms by fighting off infections and soothing irritated tissues.
Cichoric acid is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and cough.
Alkylamides are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the cough.
Caffeic acid is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the throat, thereby relieving cough symptoms.
Kaempferol is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a herbal remedy that can help soothe a cough. The plant contains powerful compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When you have a cough, the airways in your throat and lungs can become inflamed, leading to discomfort and pain. Echinacea purpurea tincture can help reduce this inflammation, making it easier to breathe and cough up mucus. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections that cause coughs. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tincture has expectorant properties, which means it can help loosen and clear out mucus from your airways. This can make it easier to cough up mucus and feel better. Some people also use Echinacea purpurea tincture to help boost their immune system, which can help prevent coughs and other illnesses.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tinctures that help with cough, start by gathering fresh or dried Goldenseal roots. You will also need a clean glass jar with a lid, a high-proof vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass dropper. First, chop the Goldenseal roots into small pieces and place them in the glass jar, filling it about 2/3 full. Next, pour the vodka or glycerin over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the liquid extract the active compounds from the roots. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Hydrastis canadensis tincture is now ready to use, and you can store it in the dark glass jar with the dropper for up to a year. To take the tincture, simply place a few drops under your tongue or mix it with water to help soothe your cough.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for cough because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion.
Quercetin is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Eucalyptol is good for cough because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.

Tannins, also known as Goldenseal, are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.
Polyphenols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing swelling and irritation that can cause coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a natural remedy for coughs. It works by loosening and thinning mucus, making it easier to cough up. The main active ingredient, eucalyptol, has a decongestant effect, reducing swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. This helps to open up airways and make breathing easier. When inhaled, eucalyptol also helps to kill bacteria and viruses that cause coughs. As a result, symptoms such as a sore throat and hoarseness are reduced. Some people also find that eucalyptus globulus tincture helps to ease a sore chest and relieve congestion. It can be taken as a liquid or added to water, making it easy to use.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture to help with cough, you will need a few basic supplies and some dried goldenseal root. First, prepare your equipment and workspace by washing your hands and ensuring a clean area to work in. Next, measure out the dried goldenseal root to the desired amount, typically around 1 part of dried root to 2 parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a glass jar. Add the dried root to the jar and fill it to the top with the solvent, making sure the root is completely covered. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for about 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the goldenseal root. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the dried root and discard the solids. Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle, and label it with the date and the contents. The tincture is now ready for use and can be taken in small amounts, typically 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day as needed to help soothe and calm a cough.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tincture, also known as thyme tincture, is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. The tincture is made from the leaves of the thyme plant, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The active ingredients in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help calm a sore throat and reduce the production of mucus. This can make it easier to cough up phlegm and other irritants that are causing the cough. Thyme tincture can also help to break up congestion in the chest and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. Some people find that thyme tincture helps to reduce the severity and frequency of coughing, making it a popular natural remedy for coughs and colds. When used in moderation, thyme tincture can be a useful addition to a treatment plan for coughs, especially those caused by respiratory infections.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture for cough relief, start by gathering fresh or dried goldenseal roots. If using fresh roots, chop them into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven or a dehydrator until they are completely dry. Next, combine the dried goldenseal roots with a carrier oil, such as vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. The general ratio for this tincture is one part goldenseal root to two parts carrier oil. Make sure the goldenseal roots are completely covered by the carrier oil, and seal the jar tightly. Now, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for two to three weeks, shaking the jar gently every day or two. This process allows the medicinal properties of the goldenseal roots to infuse into the carrier oil, creating a potent tincture. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small amounts, usually 10-20 drops, several times a day to help soothe and calm a cough.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal tinctures for cough

Saponins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help reduce irritation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to cough up mucus and soothe a sore throat.
Triterpenoids are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Polyphenols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is a natural remedy for coughs. It comes from the root of the licorice plant, which has been used for centuries to soothe sore throats and calm coughs. The main active ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways. This makes it easier to breathe and swallow, providing relief from coughing fits. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, helping to calm a cough and reduce the amount of mucus produced. By reducing inflammation and soothing the throat, Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture can provide quick and effective relief from coughs, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture to help with cough, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need 1 part of dried Goldenseal root (Hydrastis canadensis) and 2 parts of vodka or a neutral-tasting spirit. You can purchase dried Goldenseal root from a health food store or online. To make the tincture, simply combine the dried Goldenseal root and vodka in a clean glass jar. Make sure the root is completely covered by the vodka, then seal the jar tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the roots release their medicinal properties into the vodka. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in small amounts (about 20-30 drops) under the tongue, several times a day, to help soothe and calm a cough.

Shogaols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tincture, also known as ginger tincture, is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat. This makes it easier to breathe and swallow. The tincture also contains compounds that act as natural expectorants, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. When you have a cough, your body tries to get rid of irritants or infections by producing mucus. Ginger tincture helps to thin out this mucus, making it easier to cough up. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of ginger help to relax the muscles in the throat, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. This makes ginger tincture a popular natural remedy for coughs, colds, and flu.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture that helps with cough, you will need dried goldenseal root and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, prepare the dried goldenseal root by cleaning it with a small brush to remove any dirt. Then, place the root in a clean glass jar and cover it with the solvent, making sure the root is fully submerged. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration and it helps to extract the medicinal properties of the goldenseal root into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture is now ready to use as needed to help soothe a cough. You can take 10-20 drops of the tincture in water or another liquid up to 3-4 times a day, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tincture, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. The main reason for this is that peppermint contains compounds called menthol and menthone, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, these compounds can help reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful for people who have a persistent cough due to a cold or allergies. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help calm an irritated throat, making it feel more comfortable and reducing the urge to cough. Furthermore, peppermint has been traditionally used to ease digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, which can also contribute to coughing. As a result, taking peppermint tincture may help alleviate cough symptoms by addressing the underlying causes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture that helps with cough, you will need dried goldenseal root and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, measure out one part of dried goldenseal root for every two parts of the solvent, and combine them in a clean glass jar. Make sure the root is completely covered by the solvent, and then seal the jar tightly. Next, place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the roots infuse into the solvent. This process is called maceration, and it allows the active compounds in the goldenseal root to dissolve into the solvent, creating a potent tincture. After two weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken as needed to help soothe a cough, typically by mixing a few drops of the tincture with water or another liquid and drinking it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. This tincture is made from the leaves and flowers of the plant, which have been used for centuries to calm the throat and airways. The active ingredients in the tincture, such as mucilage and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and lungs. As a result, Verbascum thapsus tincture can help to break up mucus and phlegm, making it easier to cough up and clear the airways. The soothing effects of the tincture can also help to calm a cough, making it less frequent and less severe. When taken as directed, Verbascum thapsus tincture can provide quick relief from a cough, especially one caused by a cold or allergies. It can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water or tea to help ease a cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hydrastis canadensis tincture that helps with cough, start by gathering the roots of the goldenseal plant, also known as Hydrastis canadensis. Make sure to identify the plant correctly and harvest the roots in the fall, after the first frost. Next, clean and chop the roots into small pieces, then place them in a clean glass jar. Pour high-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. The general ratio is one part roots to two parts solvent. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs infuse into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to smaller bottles, label them, and store them in a cool, dark place. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water as needed to help soothe a cough. Note: Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tincture is a natural remedy for coughs. It's made from the elderberry plant, which has been used for centuries to soothe sore throats and calm coughs. The active ingredients in the tincture, such as flavonoids and sambunigrin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways. This makes it easier to breathe and swallow. The tincture also has antiviral properties, which can help fight off the underlying infection that's causing the cough. When taken as directed, Sambucus nigra tincture can provide quick relief from coughs, making it a popular choice for people of all ages. It's also gentle on the stomach and can be taken with other medications, making it a good option for those who need extra help managing their cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tinctures that help with cough, you'll need to gather dry goldenseal roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or a high-proof spirit as the solvent, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid for storing the tincture. First, chop the dry goldenseal roots into small pieces and fill the glass jar about 2/3 full. Next, pour the vodka over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Secure the lid tightly and let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration, where the solvent extracts the active compounds from the herbs. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the liquid tincture to the dark glass bottle and label it with the date, ingredients, and dosage instructions. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops of the goldenseal tincture in water or tea as needed to help soothe a cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, is a plant-based remedy used to soothe and calm a persistent cough. The tincture form of goldenseal is effective in treating coughs because it contains compounds called alkaloids, particularly berberine. Berberine has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the throat, helping to ease congestion and discomfort. It also has antimicrobial properties that fight off infections and bacteria that can cause coughs. When taken as a tincture, the berberine in goldenseal can quickly reach the bloodstream and start working to calm a cough. Additionally, goldenseal has a natural expectorant effect, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further reducing coughing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture for cough relief, start by gathering high-quality roots of the goldenseal plant, also known as Hydrastis canadensis. Clean and dry the roots thoroughly, then chop them into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction. Combine one part of the chopped goldenseal roots with one part of a carrier oil, such as glycerin or vodka, in a clean glass jar. The ratio of goldenseal roots to carrier oil is crucial, as it determines the strength of the final tincture. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking the mixture regularly to facilitate the extraction process. Allow the mixture to steep for about two to three weeks, allowing the active compounds to dissolve into the carrier oil. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) to help soothe and calm coughs, as the active compounds in goldenseal, such as berberine, have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues.
remedies.image_alt: Hydrastis canadensis herbal tinctures for cough

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be an effective way to relieve a persistent cough. Echinacea purpurea is a popular herb used to boost the immune system and fight off infections that cause coughs. It contains compounds called alkylamides that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and ease congestion. Eucalyptus globulus, on the other hand, has natural decongestant properties that help to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. By combining these two herbs in a tincture, you can create a powerful remedy for coughs. A persistent cough can be not only uncomfortable but also disrupt your daily life, making it difficult to sleep, work, or engage in activities you enjoy. Fixing the issue is essential to regain your energy and improve your overall well-being. By incorporating herbal tinctures into your routine, you can take control of your health and alleviate the symptoms of a cough.
supplementary_best: A combination of Thymus vulgaris, Eucalyptus globulus, and Mentha x piperita tinctures can be effective in relieving a cough. Thymus vulgaris (Thyme) tincture has antiviral properties that help fight off the underlying infection causing the cough. Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a decongestant that helps reduce mucus buildup in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm. Mentha x piperita (Peppermint) tincture has expectorant properties that further help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways. This combination works well together because Thymus vulgaris targets the infection, Eucalyptus globulus helps clear out the mucus, and Mentha x piperita makes it easier to expel the mucus, providing quick relief from coughing.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a cough, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that may worsen your condition or interact with medications. One such herb is Kava (Piper methysticum), which can slow down breathing and worsen respiratory problems. Another herb to avoid is Valerian (Valeriana officinalis), which can cause drowsiness and interact with sedatives, making it harder to breathe. Additionally, Ginkgo (Ginkgo biloba) should be avoided as it can cause allergic reactions and interact with blood thinners, which may worsen cough symptoms. Furthermore, Lobelia (Lobelia inflata) can cause respiratory problems and interact with medications, including sedatives and antidepressants. It's also best to avoid Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) as it can cause allergic reactions and interact with medications, including blood thinners and sedatives. Finally, Ephedra (Ephedra sinica) should be avoided as it can cause high blood pressure, heart problems, and respiratory issues, which may worsen cough symptoms.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. It has a thick, sticky texture that coats the throat and helps calm irritation. When you eat honey, it dissolves in your saliva and forms a protective barrier against the coughing. This barrier helps reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits. Honey also contains antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections that may be causing the cough.  Slippery elm is another natural remedy that can help with cough. It's a type of bark that's high in mucilage, a thick, gooey substance that helps protect and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat. When you take slippery elm, the mucilage dissolves in your saliva and forms a protective barrier that helps calm irritation and reduce inflammation in the throat. This can help make coughing less uncomfortable and less frequent.  Marshmallow root is similar to slippery elm in that it's also high in mucilage. It's been used for centuries to help soothe irritated throats and calm coughs. When you take marshmallow root, the mucilage helps form a protective barrier that reduces inflammation and irritation in the throat. This can help make coughing less painful and less frequent.  Steam inhalation is also a natural remedy that can help with cough. When you inhale steam, it helps loosen and clear mucus from your throat and lungs. This can help reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits. You can inhale steam by taking a hot shower, using a humidifier, or inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have a cough may also experience other ailments, such as congestion, sore throat, and fatigue. Congestion can be caused by the buildup of mucus in the airways, and medicinal herbs like eucalyptus and thyme can help to break it down and relieve symptoms. Eucalyptus contains compounds that have natural decongestant properties, while thyme has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat underlying infections.  A sore throat can be caused by the inflammation of the mucous membranes, and medicinal herbs like slippery elm and licorice root can help to soothe and protect them. Slippery elm has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain, while licorice root has antiviral properties that can help to combat viral infections.  Fatigue can be caused by the body's increased effort to fight off an underlying infection, and medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can help to boost energy levels. Ashwagandha has adaptogenic properties that can help the body to cope with stress and increase energy levels, while ginseng has stimulating properties that can help to increase alertness and vitality.  Additionally, people who have a cough may also experience headaches and body aches, which can be caused by the inflammation of the airways and underlying infections. Medicinal herbs like feverfew and willow bark can help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. Feverfew has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce pain and fever, while willow bark contains salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin and can help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.  These medicinal herbs work by targeting the underlying causes of these ailments, such as infections and inflammation, rather than just treating the symptoms. By using these herbs, people who have a cough may be able to experience relief from these accompanying symptoms and improve their overall well-being.
